key_highlights: Global markets experienced a broad sell-off, with stocks posting their worst day since March, driven by rising bond yields and inflation fears.
Inflation concerns intensified as business input prices spiked (headline 1), and oil prices surged, rattling bond markets globally.
Cerebras (CRBS) had a massive IPO, surging 108% on strong chip demand, marking the biggest IPO of 2026 so far.
Berkshire Hathaway amassed a $2.6 billion stake in Delta Airlines, signaling value-oriented positioning in travel recovery.
Ford fell sharply after an AI-driven rally abated, but also announced battery investments for data centers.
The bond rout deepened, with the 10-year yield rising and the dollar notching its best week since early March on Fed hike expectations.
SpaceX is reportedly planning an IPO filing as soon as Wednesday, adding to a busy IPO pipeline.
Geopolitical tensions (Iran negotiations, Trump's trip) and a Fed leadership transition (Kevin Warsh incoming) added uncertainty.

sectors.trend: Strong demand for AI chips continues, with Cerebras IPO surging 108% and Nvidia receiving bullish analyst notes. However, rising yields triggered a sell-off in chipmakers, and AI data centers are noted to employ very few people, raising questions about job displacement.
sectors.implications: AI infrastructure remains a high-growth area, but valuation sensitivity to interest rates is high. Investors should focus on companies with strong fundamentals and diversified revenue streams.

sectors.trend: Oil prices surged, contributing to global bond sell-off and inflation fears. Berkshire sold $8 billion of Chevron shares, while Enbridge and Alberta Premier discussed pipeline projects. Adnoc kept loading LNG tankers, and YPF seeks incentives for a $25 billion oil project.
sectors.implications: Energy sector benefits from higher oil prices, but regulatory and geopolitical risks remain. Investors should monitor OPEC+ decisions and Iran negotiations.

sectors.trend: Bond yields rose sharply, hitting equities and bonds. Forbright filed for IPO pitching middle market and digital banking. The Fed faces 'regime change' with Powell stepping down and Kevin Warsh taking over. Mortgage rates showed mixed moves, with 30-year and 20-year rates moving lower.
sectors.implications: Higher yields benefit banks' net interest margins but pressure bond prices. The Fed transition adds policy uncertainty. Investors should favor well-capitalized banks with diversified revenue.

sectors.trend: Ford fell most in 15 months after AI-driven rally abated, but also announced battery investments for data centers. Toyota mulls $2 billion investment in Texas assembly line and Honda unveiled hybrid prototypes. Tesla to invest $250 million in Berlin Gigafactory. VinFast seeks to shed debt.
sectors.implications: Auto sector faces mixed signals: EV demand remains strong but competition intensifies. Legacy automakers are pivoting to hybrids and data center batteries. Investors should watch for margin pressures and supply chain developments.

sectors.trend: Mortgage rates showed mixed moves, with 30-year and 20-year rates moving lower. Zillow CEO is preparing for the next revolution in real estate. HELOC rates remain higher than purchase loans.
sectors.implications: Housing market remains sensitive to rates. Lower mortgage rates could spur activity, but affordability challenges persist. Real estate tech and digital platforms may see growth.

sectors.trend: Primark makes big change to stores nationwide. Wendy's considers strategic pivot to China. DoorDash price target lowered after mixed Q1 results. McDonald's price targets lowered despite earnings beat. Subscription fatigue is alienating consumers.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending is under pressure from inflation. Discount retailers and value-oriented brands may outperform. Subscription models face headwinds.

sectors.trend: Eli Lilly price target raised. Natera's cancer recurrence tests prompt Ark Invest to expand stake. Rare Ebola strain may have killed 80 in Congo.
sectors.implications: Healthcare remains a defensive play with growth in oncology and rare diseases. Biotech innovation continues, but regulatory and geopolitical risks exist.

sectors.trend: Erock, a power systems maker for data centers, files for US IPO. NextEra Energy in talks to acquire Dominion. Building batteries for data centers re-energizes Ford stock. AI data centers employ very few people.
sectors.implications: Data center demand is booming, driving investment in power systems and batteries. However, employment impact is limited. Investors should focus on companies providing critical infrastructure.

sectors.trend: Berkshire amasses $2.6 billion stake in Delta Airlines. UPS analyst sentiment is bullish. Fuel costs driving demand to buses, says Flix North America CEO. LIRR service suspended due to electrical fire, and workers strike.
sectors.implications: Transportation sector benefits from travel demand and fuel cost shifts. Berkshire's Delta stake signals value opportunity. Labor disruptions and infrastructure issues pose risks.

sectors.trend: Emerging markets cap worst week since March on inflation fears. Korean stocks tumbled after reaching milestone. Singapore's AI strategy in global race. Argentina's YPF seeks incentives for oil project.
sectors.implications: EM assets are under pressure from rising US yields and inflation. Selective opportunities in AI and energy, but overall cautious stance warranted.

key_themes: Inflation fears resurgent: business input prices spike, oil surges, bond yields rise.
Global bond rout deepens, hitting equities and risk assets.
AI and tech IPOs remain hot: Cerebras, SpaceX, Erock, Forbright.
Fed leadership transition adds policy uncertainty.
Geopolitical risks: Iran negotiations stall, Trump's trip yields few deals, Congo Ebola outbreak.
Berkshire's value moves: buys Delta, sells Chevron.
Auto industry pivots: hybrids, data center batteries, EV investments.
Consumer spending under pressure: subscription fatigue, discount retail.
Infrastructure boom: data centers, pipelines, batteries.
Labor market tightness: migrant shortage in Spain, rail strikes in NY.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios, favor floating-rate instruments and TIPS. Increase cash allocation. Focus on companies with pricing power.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical tensions (Iran, Trump trip, Congo Ebola)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify geographically, avoid overexposure to oil-sensitive sectors. Monitor diplomatic developments.
Prepare for potential rate hikes or policy shifts. Stay agile with portfolio duration. Watch Fed speeches and economic data.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Equity valuation compression from rising yields
risk_assessment.mitigation: Rotate from high-growth, high-multiple stocks to value and dividend-paying stocks. Increase defensive exposure.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil price surge and energy supply disruptions
risk_assessment.mitigation: Overweight energy sector, but take profits after rallies. Hedge with commodity ETFs or futures.
Avoid companies with high labor cost exposure. Favor automation and AI beneficiaries. Monitor wage inflation.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer spending slowdown and subscription fatigue
risk_assessment.mitigation: Invest in discount retailers and essential goods. Avoid overvalued consumer discretionary names.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: IPO market froth and potential corrections
risk_assessment.mitigation: Be selective in IPO participation. Focus on companies with strong fundamentals and clear growth paths.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) on weakness for its value-oriented, diversified portfolio and recent Delta stake.
opportunities.rationale: Berkshire's moves (buying Delta, selling Chevron) show disciplined capital allocation. The stock offers a defensive play with upside from insurance and rail.

opportunities.recommendation: Add to AI infrastructure plays like Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD on pullbacks.
opportunities.rationale: Cerebras IPO surge and strong chip demand confirm AI capex cycle. Rising yields cause short-term pain, but long-term demand is intact.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy infrastructure and pipeline companies (Enbridge, NextEra).
opportunities.rationale: Oil price surge and data center power demand boost infrastructure plays. NextEra's potential Dominion acquisition signals consolidation.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider Delta Airlines (DAL) following Berkshire's stake.
opportunities.rationale: Berkshire's $2.6B stake signals value. Travel demand remains strong, and fuel costs are manageable with hedging.

opportunities.recommendation: Explore IPO participation in SpaceX and Erock for long-term growth.
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX's planned IPO and Erock's data center power focus align with secular trends in space and AI infrastructure.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation to 15-20% of portfolio.
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields and market volatility warrant higher cash reserves to deploy during dips. Money market rates up to 4.01% APY provide attractive yield.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge bond exposure with short-duration Treasuries or TIPS.
defensive_moves.rationale: Global bond sell-off is worsening. Short-duration bonds reduce interest rate risk. TIPS protect against inflation.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Rotate into defensive sectors: healthcare (LLY, Natera), consumer staples, and utilities.
defensive_moves.rationale: Inflation and rising yields favor defensive sectors with stable earnings. Eli Lilly and Natera have strong growth profiles.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-growth tech and consumer discretionary names.
defensive_moves.rationale: Rising yields compress valuations. Ford, DoorDash, and McDonald's are facing headwinds. Take profits on recent AI winners.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Buy gold and silver on pullbacks as a hedge against inflation and geopolitical risk.
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold and silver are headed for weekly losses but remain attractive hedges. Iran negotiations stall and inflation fears support precious metals.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Negative to cautious. Rising bond yields, inflation fears, and Fed transition will keep markets volatile. Expect further equity downside, with the S&P 500 potentially testing support levels. Defensive sectors and cash will outperform. Oil prices may remain elevated, adding to inflation concerns. IPO market remains active but selective.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Neutral to positive. Inflation may moderate as supply chains adjust and Fed policy stabilizes under new leadership. AI and infrastructure investments will drive growth. Value stocks and energy may continue to outperform. Geopolitical risks (Iran, trade) could create buying opportunities. Long-term investors should focus on quality and diversification.
key_catalysts: Fed leadership transition: Kevin Warsh's policy stance and first FOMC meeting.
Iran nuclear negotiations outcome and impact on oil prices.
Cerebras and SpaceX IPO performance and broader IPO market sentiment.
Q2 earnings season: guidance on inflation, margins, and demand.
US inflation data (CPI, PPI) and employment reports.
Global bond market stabilization or further sell-off.
Geopolitical developments: Trump's trade policy, Congo Ebola outbreak.
Oil price trajectory and OPEC+ decisions.
Consumer spending data and retail earnings.
AI regulation and antitrust developments (Musk v. Altman trial).
watch_list: 10-year US Treasury yield and 2-year yield spread.
WTI crude oil price and Brent crude.
Cerebras (CRBS) stock price and analyst coverage.
Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) 13F filings for further portfolio moves.
Fed speeches and minutes from upcoming meetings.
Ebola outbreak in Congo and global health response.
NextEra Energy (NEE) and Dominion (D) merger talks.
Ford (F) battery investment details and EV sales data.
Bitcoin and Ethereum price action as risk sentiment indicator.
